definition: Compute a ligand similarity, such as molecular fingerprint Tanimoto, and a target similarity, such as protein sequence identity or embedding cosine. Combine the two pairwise similarity files with a conservative minimum or weighted mean so that a test compound-target pair is considered close to training only when the relevant scientific components are close.
example_usage: python composite_pairwise_similarity.py --inputs ligand_pairwise.csv target_pairwise.csv --method min --out pairwise_similarity.csv

citations.title: Rethinking the generalization of drug target affinity prediction algorithms via similarity aware evaluation

evidence.summary: The paper defines pairwise sample similarity, aggregates similarity to the training set, and uses Morgan fingerprints with Tanimoto in the main example.
The appendix compares multiple fingerprint and similarity-measure choices for similarity-aware evaluation.

citations.title: BASE: a web service for providing compound-protein binding affinity prediction datasets with reduced similarity bias

evidence.summary: The paper computes sequence, functional, and integrated target-protein similarities and filters training data by similarity cutoffs.

limitations: The right composition rule depends on whether novelty is ligand-driven, target-driven, or interaction-driven.
Ligand-only similarity can miss protein-family leakage; target-only similarity can miss analog series leakage.
Pairwise matrices can be large and may require sparse storage for million-scale libraries.
quality_gates: Report component similarities and the composition rule.
Plot separate ligand-only, target-only, and composite SPECTRA curves.
Check that repeated compounds, repeated targets, and assay identifiers are handled explicitly.
